---
title: "A preliminary study of a camp-based dynamic contemplative course on children’s sustained attention and balance"
author: "Yeh, C.-W., Lien, Y.-W., Chen, R.-Y., Chuang, P.-Y., Cheng, W.-H., & Chen, H.-L."
image: "../../assets/study-covers-en/2023-yeh-camp-attention-balance.png"
lang: en
language: ../../assets/language-en.yml
toc-title: "On this page"
---

```{r}
#| include: false
source("../../R/bmaa_plot_theme.R")
```

::: {.study-cover}
<img src="../../assets/study-covers-en/2023-yeh-camp-attention-balance.png" alt="Six-day camp-based BMAA improvement summary">

Cover figure: among five children, balance improved in the largest number of cases.
:::

## Method

A case-series pretest-posttest study with five children participating in a six-day camp-based BMAA dynamic contemplative course.

## Key Findings

This case-series study focuses on how many of the five children showed improvement rather than on group averages. Sustained attention means staying focused over time; balance reflects bodily postural control.

The figure shows the number of children who improved. Balance improved in the largest number of children, while visual and auditory sustained attention improved in some children.

```{r}
#| fig-height: 4.8
dat <- data.frame(
  outcome = c("Visual sustained attention", "Auditory sustained attention", "Balance"),
  group = "Improved cases",
  value = c(2, 1, 4)
)
bmaa_bar_plot(dat, "Six-day camp-based BMAA: improvement summary", ylab = "Number improved (out of 5)")
```

## Reference

葉淨維, 連韻文, 陳人瑜, 莊博雅, 鄭惟馨, & 陳顥齡. (2023). 營隊式動態靜觀課程對兒童持續注意力與平衡能力影響之初探. *台灣職能治療研究與實務, 19*(1), 81-97.
